title: The Cities after EOE
wikitext:
[]: The Cities after EOE?             Quote Post by destinydueler » Fri Nov 04, 2011 1:05 am So I'm recently knew to the evangelion series (and this forum). I've become an instant fan and done tons of research, although two things still nag at me. Did all the cities get entirely destroyed during EOE? Is there nothing left for Asuka and Shinji to return to. The other thing is did Misato get turned to lcl and there for able to return and does she have motivation to do so. I saw her jacket floating in LCL so I assumed she was merged with the rest of the souls though I'd like other opinions she was my favorite character and it pains me to think she wouldn't be able to come back, be brutally honest I can take it.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Nov 04, 2011 1:23 am The only real answer is that we actually don't know. It seems logical that Misato would have been swept up in Third Impact and turned into LCL, given that Asuka and Ritsuko were. Who comes back and why is anyone's guess, depending on their level of optimism.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 04, 2011 1:44 am esselfortium wrote: Who comes back and why is anyone's guess, depending on their level of optimism.

[]:             Quote Post by destinydueler » Fri Nov 04, 2011 1:52 am Well as long as there's that possibility it makes me feel better haha when I first saw EOE I kinda got depressed, but from what I've seen the series does that to quite a few people.

[]:             Quote Post by Titanium » Fri Nov 04, 2011 1:54 am Good questions. Now do keep in mind that people CAN reject instramentality, and will revert to thier original state. So, if Misato wanted to leave said state, she could. Now, wether or not she would return with her USP and clothes is a different matter. The cities with in the 3rd impact strike zone would have been flattened; but the cities halfway around the world would have just seen some orange Tang on the floor, not to mention a nice view of GNR and the Milky Way.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 04, 2011 2:33 am There is no doubt that pulling the GeoFront out of the Earth's crust will have made a serious mess of the Hakone region, implausible leaning towers in the final scene notwithstanding. Whether we are to infer continental scale devastation from a supervolcano event when the ocean pours into the crater along with a dinosaur-killer level impact event from collapsing GNR bits -- spreading the devastation all around the Pacific, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Cruel Thesis » Fri Nov 04, 2011 3:30 am I would like to think there is some hope for Shinji and Asuka at the end.. but reading through the "EoE the aftermath" topic… well yeah..

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Fri Nov 04, 2011 6:39 am I think that all those posts about how awfully bad the future will be after 3I are kind of out of synch with the author's intent. Eva is a story where enormous apocalyptic things happen but people who didn't get too unlucky more or less go on as usual. Otherwise, why would any sane civillian even remain in Tokyo-3 after the first few angel attacks (having nowhere to go isn't a good reason, as most people would prefer being homeless to being squashed by giant monstrosities)? If civilization is shown to have been rebuilt (in an eyeblink) once, it will clearly be easier to rebuild it again.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Nov 04, 2011 7:23 am Korgan wrote: I think that all those posts about how awfully bad the future will be after 3I are kind of out of synch with the author's intent.

[]: WE WILL KEEP ON, KEEPIN' ON! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Nov 04, 2011 11:56 am Mr. Tines wrote: There is no doubt that pulling the GeoFront out of the Earth's crust will have made a serious mess of the Hakone region, implausible leaning towers in the final scene notwithstanding. Whether we are to infer continental scale devastation from a supervolcano event

[]: Re: WE WILL KEEP ON, KEEPIN' ON! BD             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 04, 2011 3:00 pm Alaska Slim wrote: I know you've crunched the numbers, but isn't this just a tad overblown?

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Fri Nov 04, 2011 3:52 pm Misato definitely died. I don't have it with me at the moment but I'm pretty sure if you look closely you can see her body getting destroyed in the explosion. And yet she is in instrumentality so, I don't know if she can come back or not. Instrumentality is pretty vague and I'm pretty sure it was intended to be confusing in that sense.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Nov 04, 2011 4:27 pm Asuka died and came back. So can Misato.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 04, 2011 4:35 pm Yeah, this is a settled question. All the dead folks can come back if they care to.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 04, 2011 5:51 pm Alaska Slim wrote: What, the (2nd) EVA-01 blast?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 04, 2011 6:11 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It's comparable in terms of the energy being dumped

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Nov 05, 2011 6:42 am Just a question, while we're on this topic: Would GNR falling have any effect on the orbit, rotation, or gravity of Earth?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Nov 05, 2011 6:52 am No.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Nov 05, 2011 9:35 am Bagheera wrote: Yeah, this is a settled question. All the dead folks can come back if they care to.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Nov 05, 2011 9:50 am Stryker wrote: ABRAHAM LINCOLN! WHERE ARE YOOOOU!!!!!
